subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, IDB, Freedom House, RSF, TI) + international and Surinamese press.

A genuine post-default turnaround (the first IMF program Suriname ever completed, March 2025; debt restructured, hyperinflation tamed) sitting under a transformative offshore-oil tail (the GranMorgu Block 58 project, FID 2024, first oil ~2028).

Standout features: high-variance upside (a Guyana-style oil windfall) against a fragile near-term - the new Simons government has already let fiscal/monetary discipline slip - and weak, corruption-prone institutions.

economic.notes: A post-default stabilization now slipping, under a transformative but not-yet-realized oil tail.

fiscal_state.notes: Gross debt ~106% of GDP after a 2025 slippage reversed program gains, with an IMF call for a policy reset; oil revenue from ~2028 could transform the trajectory. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: CBvS autonomy was strengthened under the EFF but is unproven - an insufficiently restrictive 2025 stance let inflation and the SRD slip. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The SRD is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Down from post-default hyperinflation but back above 10% in 2025 after slippage; an IMF path targets ~7% by H1 2027. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Highly dollarized, thin, recovering from the crisis - governance improved under the EFF but the sector is small and vulnerable. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Open economy on gold plus nascent oil; GranMorgu is a major positive trajectory but a textbook resource-curse risk caps the near-term score. (Staatsolie; TotalEnergies; IMF)

institutional.notes: A functioning democracy with real judicial reach but pervasive corruption and low capacity.

rule_of_law.notes: A constitutional democracy with functioning courts (the Bouterse conviction is real evidence of reach) but pervasive corruption, clientelism, and weak enforcement. (Freedom House 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Improved and IMF-cited under the EFF, but capacity is thin - treat official data as partially entangled. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Low technocratic capacity, politicized appointments, and a clientelist legacy, with some EFF-built macro muscle. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Diverse media and few attacks (RSF ~31st, 2025), but a harsh defamation law induces self-censorship. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A peaceful 2025 transfer (the first woman president) in a famously plural consociational society.

political_stability.notes: A peaceful, competitive transfer after the May 2025 election - the first woman president leads a broad NDP coalition - stable but coalition-fragile. (IPU; Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Free', generally respected, with the defamation law and uneven due process as drags. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No notable exit-ban or mass-deportation regime; modest, informal migration handling. (Freedom House)

social_cohesion.notes: A famously plural society (Hindustani, Maroon, Creole, Javanese, Indigenous) with a long consociational record; cocaine-transit crime and economic frustration are the drags. (Freedom House; InSight Crime)

demographics.notes: Relatively young with a favorable dependency ratio, but persistent emigration (especially to the Netherlands) drains skills. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned, ex-Dutch CARICOM actor courting Western oil majors, with a dormant Guyana border dispute.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned, with ex-Dutch ties, CARICOM membership, and Western oil-major courtship - no coercive dependency but limited leverage. (IMF; CARICOM)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict; a long-dormant border dispute with Guyana is managed and unlikely to escalate. (Freedom House)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not sanctioned, but FX-auction distortions and a managed regime mean de-facto frictions; AML/cocaine-corridor exposure is a watch-item. (IMF; INCSR)

physical_practical.notes: Outside the hurricane belt but flood-exposed, with weak interior services.

climate.notes: ~87% of the population on a low coastal plain below/near sea level - severe SLR plus flooding. (WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Limited, concentrated in Paramaribo, sparse in the interior, with chronic financing strain. (PAHO/WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Weak power and transport with minimal interior reach; oil revenue could fund upgrades long-term. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate homicide but a significant and rising cocaine-transit problem and weak border control. (INCSR; InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Outside the hurricane belt (a relative plus) but flood-exposed with shallow insurance penetration. (hazard context)

summary: A genuine post-default turnaround - the first IMF program Suriname ever completed (March 2025), with debt restructured and inflation brought down from hyperinflation - sitting under a transformative offshore-oil tail (the GranMorgu FID 2024, first oil ~2028) that could deliver a Guyana-style windfall. But the near-term is shakier than the headline: the new Simons government has already let fiscal and monetary discipline slip, pushing inflation back above 10% and debt to ~106% of GDP, prompting an IMF call for a policy reset. Institutions remain the binding weakness - pervasive corruption, low civil-service capacity, and a textbook resource-curse risk as oil money meets a fragile state. The skew is positive and high-variance: large upside if the windfall is well-governed, a real downside if it is not.
